title: Fluffy Malaysian Tempeh Bao
description: A sour and fluffy Malaysian dish featuring tempeh, lamination-puff, and jain-friendly ingredients, perfect for a monsoon day. Serve warm with sweet soy sauce for a delicious treat.
instructions: Mix the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sugar in a small bowl to make a marinade.
Cut the tempeh into small cubes and soak in the marinade for at least 30 minutes.
Roll out the laminated puff pastry and cut into small squares.
Place a spoonful of the tempeh mixture onto one half of each pastry square.
Fold the pastry in half and press the edges to seal the bao.
Brush the tops with water and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Place the bao on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15 minutes.
While the bao is baking, mix the tofu with water to make a sauce.
Remove the bao from the oven and brush with the tofu sauce.
Serve warm with additional tofu sauce for dipping.
